next_steps: Quantitative serum immunoglobulin levels (IgG, IgA, IgM)
Dihydrorhodamine (DHR) flow cytometry assay to evaluate for CGD
Assessment of specific antibody titers to vaccine antigens (pneumococcal, tetanus)
Next-generation sequencing for primary immunodeficiency panel
Repeat liver biopsy with dedicated tissue for specialized microbiology (metagenomic next-generation sequencing) and immunohistochemistry
Evaluation for occult malignancy or lymphoma via PET/CT

differential_diagnoses: Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated Granulomatous-Lymphocytic Interstitial Lung Disease (GLILD)
Late-onset Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD) (e.g., autosomal recessive)
STAT3 gain-of-function mutation or other primary immunodeficiency with immune dysregulation
Occult T-cell large granular lymphocyte (LGL) leukemia
Disseminated mycobacterial or atypical bacterial infection in the setting of acquired hypogammaglobulinemia
Sarcoidosis with secondary bacterial colonization of necrotic/cystic liver lesions
next_steps: Measurement of quantitative immunoglobulins (IgG, IgA, IgM) and IgG subclasses
Comprehensive T-, B-, and NK-cell lymphocyte subset analysis via flow cytometry
Evaluation of specific antibody response to protein and polysaccharide vaccines (pneumococcal, tetanus)
Metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) of liver tissue and blood for broad-spectrum pathogen detection
Whole-exome sequencing or targeted primary immunodeficiency gene panel
Serum protein electrophoresis and immunofixation to rule out monoclonal gammopathy
